sections.title: Why is Bone a Living Tissue?
points: It Grows: Increases in length and thickness as an organism matures.
It Heals: Can repair itself after a fracture or break.
It Breathes: Living cells require oxygen and nutrients brought by blood vessels.
It Senses: Contains nerves to detect pain and pressure.

sections.title: Layers of a Bone
points: Periosteum: The thin, tough outer membrane that covers the bone. It contains blood vessels and nerves.
Compact (Hard) Bone: The dense, heavy layer that gives the bone its strength and protects the marrow.
Spongy Bone: A light, porous layer found at the ends. It houses the Red Bone Marrow.
Cartilage: A smooth, flexible tissue on bone ends at the joints to prevent friction and absorb shock.

rows.valB: Can convert to Red marrow if blood is lost

sections.title: Chemical Composition
points: Bones are made of Organic matter (protein fibers for flexibility) and Inorganic matter (minerals for hardness).
Warning: Without enough organic matter, bones become brittle and snap easily! Without minerals, they become soft and bend.

sections.text: Bones act as a Mineral Bank. If blood calcium levels drop, the body 'borrows' minerals from the skeleton, which can weaken the bones if not replaced by a good diet!

cards.fact: The outer skin of the bone that brings in blood and nerves.
The hardest part of the bone used for support.
Red marrow makes blood; Yellow marrow stores fat.
